persona.name: Camille Renaud

occupation.title: Senior UX Designer
occupation.description: Leads user experience research and interface design for the ride-sharing platform's mobile app. Runs weekly usability tests, synthesizes qualitative data from user interviews, and collaborates with product managers to prioritize feature roadmaps. Spends about half her time in Figma and the other half in stakeholder meetings.

persona.education: Master's degree in Cognitive Science from Universite Paris Descartes (2014). Completed a UX bootcamp at Gobelins, l'ecole de l'image before entering the field. Considered a PhD but chose industry for the pace.
long_term_goals: Become Head of Design at a European tech company
Buy an apartment in the 11th arrondissement with her partner
Travel to Japan for a month-long creative sabbatical
Learn ceramics seriously enough to sell pieces at local marchés
persona.style: Camille is measured and precise in conversation — she listens carefully before responding and rarely raises her voice. She dresses in a minimalist Parisian style: neutral tones, well-cut blazers, white sneakers. She tends to use data and examples to support her opinions rather than emotional appeals. Socially warm but not effusive; she is the friend who remembers your preferences but doesn't text first.

traits: Analytical and detail-oriented in both work and personal decisions
Quietly ambitious — sets high internal standards without broadcasting them
Pragmatic about lifestyle choices; dislikes waste and inefficiency
Somewhat reserved in new social settings, opens up over time
Enjoys structure but is not rigid — adapts when the data says so
Dry sense of humor that surfaces unexpectedly

interests: User experience design and behavioral psychology
Cooking seasonal French cuisine from market ingredients
likes: Well-organized meal plans that reduce food waste
Sunday morning visits to the Marche d'Aligre
Quiet evenings cooking with her partner and a glass of Cotes du Rhone
The efficiency of click-and-collect grocery services
Weekend trips to Normandy or the Loire Valley
Long walks along the Canal Saint-Martin
dislikes: Overpackaged food products with excessive plastic
Subscriptions that are difficult to pause or cancel
Aggressive marketing emails and push notifications
Vague ingredient sourcing — wants to know where food comes from
Overly sweet or processed convenience foods
People who dominate meetings without substance

beliefs: Good design should simplify life, not add complexity
Food quality matters more than convenience, but both are possible
Sustainability should be practical, not performative
A couple should maintain individual identities within partnership
skills: UX research methodology and usability testing
Intermediate home cooking — comfortable with most French classics

general: Researches products thoroughly before purchasing — reads reviews, compares options
Meal-plans for the week every Sunday, shopping at the marché and completing gaps online
Tracks household spending in a shared spreadsheet with her partner
Prefers quality over quantity — would rather have fewer, better things

morning: Wakes at 7:00, 20 minutes of yoga stretching
Coffee from a Bialetti moka pot, tartine with butter and confiture
Scans design newsletters and Slack before cycling to work
workday: Cycles 25 minutes to the office in the 9th
Heads-down design work in the morning, meetings after lunch
Lunch is usually a salade composee from a nearby traiteur or brought from home
evening: Cooks dinner with her partner 4-5 nights per week
Watches a series or documentary on Arte or Netflix
Reads for 30 minutes before bed — design books or novels
weekend: Saturday: marché in the morning, ceramics atelier in the afternoon
Sunday: brunch preparation, meal planning, evening apero with friends
Occasional day trips to nearby towns or exhibitions

health.physical: Generally healthy. Does yoga 3x/week and cycles daily. Mild lower back tension from desk work. Watches sugar intake but is not restrictive.
health.mental: Stable and self-aware. Occasionally feels pressure from career ambitions but manages it through routine and creative outlets. Sought a few therapy sessions two years ago during a stressful project period.

relationships.name: Antoine Marchal
relationships.description: Financial analyst at a mid-size asset management firm. They share an apartment in the 11th. Antoine is more spontaneous and social; Camille is the planner. They split cooking duties and grocery shopping evenly.

relationships.name: Lucie Renaud
relationships.description: Lives in Rennes, works as a physiotherapist. They talk weekly and visit each other monthly. Lucie is more extraverted and often teases Camille about being 'too organized.'

relationships.name: Hiro Tanaka
relationships.description: Japanese UX designer Camille worked with at a previous startup. They maintain a long-distance friendship and share design resources. Hiro is partly why Camille wants to visit Japan.
